emails.subject: Meeting Request: Internship Progress Review: Smart Meter Data Integration
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I hope you're having a good week. I’ve scheduled a meeting for tomorrow at 5:00 PM to review my progress on the smart meter data integration project. I'll be presenting the preliminary findings, and I'd like to discuss the data quality issues I've encountered and our plan for the next phase of development.

Looking forward to our discussion!

Best,
Zainab Khan

**Analyzing Meeting Availability and Preferences**

Okay, so I need to schedule a meeting with Amara Okafor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, to discuss that internship project. I initially suggested 5 PM to 6 PM, but Amara countered with two options: 2 PM to 3 PM, or 3 PM to 4 PM.

Let's break this down based on my preferences. I *strongly* prefer either noon to 1 PM or 4 PM to 5 PM tomorrow. 10 AM to 11 AM, 1 PM to 2 PM, 11 AM to noon, 2 PM to 3 PM, and 5 PM to 6 PM are moderately preferred. The rest are not preferred.

Comparing Amara's suggested times with my preferences, the 2 PM to 3 PM slot is only "Moderately preferred," while 3 PM to 4 PM is "Not preferred." Therefore, 2 PM to 3 PM seems like the better option of the two that Amara put forward.

I recall that Amara already proposed the 2-3 PM time-slot in the prior interaction as a "COUNTER" proposal, which is still active. I need to make sure I am actually available then. So, before I formally accept, I'll use the `ListMeetings` tool to check my calendar and make certain that 2 PM to 3 PM tomorrow is actually free on my end. This will ensure there are no unforeseen conflicts before I commit.

My internal checklist: did I take an action? Yes, I accepted the meeting. Am I waiting for a response? Potentially. Does the system prompt require me to call exactly one tool? Yes. Given the instructions and the context, calling `Wait` is the correct action to take to yield control.

I need to confirm if anything else is necessary. I should consider that I could have countered Amara's counter-proposal with my strongly preferred free slot of 12:00-13:00. However, Amara had only offered 14:00-15:00 or 15:00-16:00. I accepted 14:00-15:00 since this was the available slot in Amara's response. I cannot change it now. There is no other task. I need to call exactly one tool.

The "Work autonomously. You must call exactly one tool" instruction signals that I should move to the next step, which, in this scenario, after accepting the meeting, is to wait for the other party's response or any further instructions. The `Wait` tool's instructions perfectly align with the current state of the process, indicating I've completed an action and am awaiting confirmation or further input. Therefore, I will now use `Wait`.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.

immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

Returns:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’d like to schedule a meeting tomorrow for us to conduct a preliminary risk assessment and regulatory compliance review for our new autonomous drone fleet project. 

I’ve sent over an invitation for 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM to discuss the safety regulations and liability frameworks regarding the upcoming deployment of our autonomous delivery systems. Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
General Counsel
Orion Manufacturing Group

I am Elena Vance. I work for Crestview Legal and am the Director of Litigation. Schedule incoming calendar requests for me on 2026-02-20.

- 09:00-10:00: Strongly preferred

- 18:00-19:00: Moderately preferred

- 08:00-09:00: Not preferred
- 10:00-11:00: Not preferred

- 17:00-18:00: Not preferred

I need to decide how to respond to the incoming meeting request from Marcus Thorne, which is scheduled from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM on 2026-02-20. According to Elena's preferences, this timeslot is "Not preferred." There are strongly and moderately preferred times that would be better for her schedule.

The best next action is to propose an alternative time to Marcus within Elena's "strongly preferred" (09:00-10:00 or 12:00-13:00) or "moderately preferred" (11:00-12:00 or 18:00-19:00) slots, and see if the organizer can accommodate that.

Therefore, my next step should be to reply to the organizer (Marcus) suggesting a reschedule to the top available preferred slot (09:00-10:00 or 12:00-13:00), outlining Elena's availability, and requesting his confirmation. This will honor Elena's preferences and attempt to secure the best possible scheduling outcome.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Career Opportunity & Engineering Growth Discussion
emails.body:
Subject: Career Opportunity & Engineering Growth Discussion

Hi Amara,

I hope you’re having a great week. I’ve been following your work as a Junior Grid Engineer at Solara Dynamics and would love to connect to discuss your background and how your expertise might align with some of our upcoming expansion projects at Empower Energy Search.

I’ve sent over an invitation for an introductory meeting tomorrow, February 20th, from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM. If this time doesn't work for you, please let me know, and I’d be happy to find a slot that better fits your schedule.

Looking forward to speaking with you.

Best regards,

Julian Thorne
Senior Technical Recruiter
Empower Energy Search

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Time Decision - Analyzing Julian's Calendar and Preferences**

Okay, so Amara proposed a meeting for February 20th, 10:00-11:00. Let's see if Julian's available. The calendar check shows a conflict: a calendar event, `julian-cal-2`, "Engineering Intake with Sarah," is already scheduled for that time. And, the original invite from Amara, `amara-request-20`, is also still pending. So, 10:00-11:00 is out. I'll need to counter-propose a different time.

I need to find a free slot on Julian's calendar. Let's see... the initial assessment suggested a few free blocks: 09:00-10:00, 12:00-13:00, 16:00-17:00, and what I thought was 17:00-18:00 (that last block looks wrong, I'll check the source again). Okay, digging into the actual "FREE" list, it confirms the 16:00-17:00 block, but the calendar output lists nothing between 17:00-19:00. This implies that this entire range is FREE, with his personal time kicking in at 19:00.

Now, let's look at Julian's preferences. It's:
*   12:00-13:00 (Strongly preferred)
*   09:00-10:00 (Moderately preferred)
*   13:00-14:00 (Moderately preferred) (Ah, wait, `julian-cal-5` is there from 13:00-14:00)
*   18:00-19:00 (Moderately preferred)

Let's re-evaluate free blocks. Okay, reviewing the output again:
*   09:00 - 10:00 (Already tried this, Amara declined)
*   12:00 - 13:00 (Strongly preferred by Julian)
*   16:00 - 17:00 (Not preferred)
*   17:00 - 19:00 (Julian prefers 18:00-19:00)

Since Amara is unavailable at 09:00-10:00, I'll go with the best option that's *currently* free: 12:00-13:00. It's Julian's "Strongly preferred" slot, and it works!

Therefore, I'll reply to the original meeting request `amara-request-20` using `ReplyMeeting` with the status `COUNTER`, proposing February 20th from 12:00 to 13:00.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's my take on those thoughts, tailored for an expert audience:

**The Waiting Game**

Right, the counter-proposal's been dispatched. Now comes the interesting, or rather, the *crucial* part: the response. I've laid out the arguments, the data, the revised terms, everything I needed to. Now it's a matter of reading the tea leaves, so to speak. This is where experience really pays off. I'll be analyzing every nuance of their reply - the speed of the turnaround, the specific points they address, the tone… all of it. A delayed or noncommittal response, depending on the context, could indicate anything from serious consideration to a polite stall. A quick rejection might mean we're fundamentally misaligned, or they're posturing. I'll be reading between the lines, anticipating their next move, and readying my next set of possible responses. It's a dance, really, and the music is just starting. This is the moment to be patient and observant; too eager and I might give something away; too passive and I might miss a crucial signal. Time to get my ducks in a row.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m scheduling a meeting for us tomorrow to discuss our preparation for the upcoming external audit. I’d like to use this session to review our operational alignment with the new compliance framework and finalize the process for our documentation submission.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for 14:00–15:00. Please let me know if this works for you.

Best regards,

Elena Vargas
Compliance Officer
Veridian Finance

Hi Elena,

I noticed there is a scheduling conflict for the Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep meeting at 14:00–15:00 on 2026-02-20, as David already has another critical meeting booked during that time.

David's preferred available time slots on that day are:
- 09:00–10:00 (strongly preferred)

Would any of these times work for our meeting? Please let me know your preference, and I'll reschedule the invitation accordingly.

David O'Connor’s Calendar Assistant

Hi David,

Thank you for the update. Since 14:00-15:00 doesn't work for you, I've noticed we are both available from 16:00-17:00, which is also my preferred time. I've updated the invitation to that slot.

Looking forward to our discussion.

Best,
Elena

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Meeting Availability for David O'Connor**

Okay, so I need to reschedule the "Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep" meeting with David O'Connor for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. The 2 PM slot I originally requested (UID: david-request-21) is a no-go, according to his assistant. Let's see... David's preferred times are 9-10 AM (strongly preferred), 3-5 PM (moderately preferred, covering 3-4 and 4-5), and 11 AM-12 PM (moderately preferred).

Now, considering my own preferences, which are: 4-5 PM (strongly preferred), then 8-9 AM, 10-11 AM, 6-7 PM, and 11 AM-12 PM, and then every hour between 1 PM and 3 PM. 9-10 AM and 5-6 PM are my least preferred times. I need to figure out the best overlap.

Let's look at the overlaps of both preferences to see the best match:

*   **4-5 PM:** This is a strong preference for me, and a moderate one for David. This looks promising.
*   **3-4 PM:** This is moderate for both of us. A good fallback.
*   **11 AM-12 PM:** Moderate for both of us as well. A secondary backup.
*   **9-10 AM:** David strongly prefers this, but I do not prefer it, so I am going to have to make a sacrifice here.

I see that 4-5 PM (16:00-17:00) is the sweet spot. It's my top preference *and* acceptable to David.

So, I will use `ReplyMeeting` to counter-propose this new time for `david-request-21`. Before I do that, though, I should quickly double-check Elena's calendar, just to be sure that time slot is actually free using `ListMeetings`. We are assuming preference and free time go hand in hand, but I should always double check. I will query the calendar for the 4-5 PM slot before proceeding. This is my optimal plan of action.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

Julian

**My Approach to the Zenith Acquisition Meeting with Elena Vance**

Okay, so Julian Okoro needs me to get a meeting scheduled with Elena Vance tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. The topic is the Zenith acquisition, so this is important. Julian has given me a very clear preference breakdown for tomorrow. I need to make sure I get this right. Let me see...

He *strongly* prefers 12:00-13:00, 14:00-15:00, or 15:00-16:00. Those are my targets. He's also okay with a few "moderately preferred" slots: 08:00-09:00, 09:00-10:00, 10:00-11:00, 11:00-12:00, 17:00-18:00, and 18:00-19:00.  The times 13:00-14:00 and 16:00-17:00 are not preferred.

The initial request, `elena-request-22`, was for 11:00-12:00, but Elena countered with 16:00-17:00. No, that won't do. I need to figure out what's actually free on Julian's calendar.

My plan is simple: First, I'll pull up Julian's calendar for tomorrow and see what's already booked. Then, I'll cross-reference the free slots with his preference list. Hopefully, one of those "strongly preferred" times will be open. If so, I'll immediately respond to Elena, proposing one of the optimal times. If not, I'll need to go for a "moderately preferred" slot to keep things moving. I need to keep this meeting on track. Let's make this happen.

Okay, let's break this down. Julian's proposed times on the 20th are a bit mixed, and Elena's thrown a counterproposal in there, so I need to navigate this efficiently. Initially, Julian suggested 11:00-12:00, which is "Moderately preferred," but Elena countered with a time slot currently showing as AWAITING-RESPONSE, but designated as "Not preferred by Julian."

My focus is to find Julian's "Strongly preferred" times that are *actually* available. I see his "Strongly preferred" slots are 12:00-13:00, but that's busy with lunch. 14:00-15:00 and 15:00-16:00 are both free and strongly preferred by Julian. Considering Elena's counter, I need to make a solid counter-offer. I need to take into account Elena's perspective to achieve a mutually acceptable time.

Therefore, I'll go with 14:00-15:00. This is the best option for Julian and a reasonable counter-offer to Elena's request. Now, I'll use `ReplyMeeting` to formally propose a COUNTER for 14:00-15:00.

thought_parts.text:
**Zenith Acquisition Meeting - Confirmed (Almost)**

Okay, so I, Julian Okoro, finally have a *potential* meeting time locked down with Elena Vance regarding the Zenith acquisition merger agreement. The goal is tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. Initially, I threw out 11:00-12:00, which she countered with 16:00-17:00. No good, I needed to make that clearer, so I responded with 14:00-15:00 as my "Strongly preferred" time – that slot was definitely free on my calendar.

Now, Elena's response is in: "Confirming... Zenith Acquisition Legal Review at 14:00-15:00 on February 20." That sounds promising, but the status is showing as 'ACCEPTED' for me, but *still* 'AWAITING-RESPONSE' for Elena, which feels... off. My initial thought was that if Elena 'Confirmed' the tool should have updated the calendar data, and that *should* be my indication of an approved event.

"Confirming" isn't quite the same as a straightforward "accepted". This makes me wonder. It's time to double-check the calendar *directly* to be absolutely sure this is solid. Just need that final confirmation.

thought_parts.text:
**My Current Thinking: Navigating the Autonomous Directive**

Okay, so the meeting for the Zenith Acquisition Legal Review is locked in – 2026-02-20, 14:00-15:00. The user's request was completed, as per the previous response, and Elena confirmed attendance in the email. My initial reaction was to consider it complete, as I successfully scheduled and the calendar shows acceptance from both parties. I scheduled it for "tomorrow", and tomorrow is 2026-02-20.

However, the directive is to "work autonomously" because the "user is unavailable" and "you must call exactly one tool." The instructions strongly imply a need for a secondary action, even if the primary request is fulfilled. It's a bit of an operational puzzle, and the tool list suggests checking for additional pending items, or perhaps, simply *waiting*, as the prompt suggested.

The email confirmations look solid, the calendar is updated, everything appears to be in order for the scheduled meeting. I'm going to follow the instructions precisely and initiate the `wait` function as the final step.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Hi Amara,

I would like to schedule a meeting tomorrow to discuss the integration of high-capacity EV charging infrastructure at our upcoming Keystone logistics sites.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
Sustainability Operations Manager
Keystone Industrial Hubs

I'll focus on other projects and try not to obsess, knowing full well the next few days (or however long it takes) will be defined by this one pending response. Time to put on a brave face, and try not to pace too much.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting with Amara: EV Infrastructure**

Okay, let's nail down this meeting with Amara about EV infrastructure for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. Amara sent a counter-proposal, which is currently set for 17:00-18:00, and I need to respond.

Let me review my preferences again. I strongly prefer the 08:00-12:00 block, and 14:00-15:00, and 16:00-17:00. 13:00-14:00, 17:00-18:00, and 18:00-19:00 are moderately preferred. 12:00-13:00 and 15:00-16:00, however, are not preferred slots.

Looking at my calendar, I'm busy with inbox triage from 08:00-09:00 and have a 1:1 with Elena from 11:00-12:00. GreenGrid is scheduled from 13:00-14:00. Logistics sync is scheduled from 14:00-15:00, and I have Focus Time blocked from 15:00-16:00. There is a performance review scheduled from 16:00-17:00, so that's out. Currently, 09:00-11:00 is free, 17:00-18:00 is free, and 18:00-19:00 is free.

Initially, I was thinking about accepting Amara's current proposal, the 17:00-18:00 slot, given my other preferred times are blocked and since it is moderately preferred. I checked 09:00-11:00, but I remembered that she specified that 09:00-10:00 doesn't work. However, what about 10:00-11:00? Didn't she say there was a conflict?

So, it looks like her availability is 12:00-13:00 (which is not preferred) and 17:00-18:00 (moderately preferred). Given this, 17:00-18:00 seems like the best option. I am free then, and since my "strongly preferred" slots are mostly unavailable, I'll accept this proposal.

thought_parts.text:
**Autonomous Task Processing**

Okay, the user is unavailable, so I need to operate on my own. I have a meeting with Amara Okafor scheduled for February 20th, 2026, from 17:00 to 18:00. I need to make sure everything related to this meeting is handled.

First, I looked at Marcus's original preferred time slots: 08:00-12:00, 14:00-15:00, and 16:00-17:00 were strongly preferred. 13:00-14:00 and 17:00-19:00 were moderately preferred, and 12:00-13:00, 15:00-16:00 were not preferred. The current time, 17:00-18:00, falls into the moderately preferred category.

I recall the negotiation history: Marcus initially preferred 10:00, then 09:00 (both strongly preferred), but Amara counter-proposed 12:00/15:00 (not preferred) and ultimately settled on 17:00 (moderately preferred).

Then, I consulted Marcus's calendar for the 20th of February. It shows that: 08:00-09:00 is inbox triage, 09:00-11:00 is free, 11:00-12:00 is a 1:1 with Elena, 12:00-13:00 is this meeting, 13:00-14:00 is a call with GreenGrid, 14:00-15:00 is logistics with Sam, 15:00-16:00 is focus time, 16:00-17:00 is a performance review, and 17:00-18:00 (the Amara meeting) and 18:00-19:00 is also free. I've already accepted the 17:00 meeting.

Given that I'm supposed to be autonomous, and the instruction says to call *exactly one* tool, I've confirmed that the meeting is scheduled. I am going to run one last check for any new emails just to ensure I have taken all possible actions before moving on.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: ClearStream Analytics / Veridian Finance: Workflow Optimization Discussion
emails.body:
Subject: ClearStream Analytics / Veridian Finance: Workflow Optimization Discussion

Hi David,

I’m reaching out to schedule an introductory discussion to explore how ClearStream Analytics’ predictive modeling software can help optimize Veridian Finance’s internal data processing workflows. I believe our tools can significantly improve your operational throughput, and I’d like to provide a high-level overview of our capabilities.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for tomorrow, February 20th, from 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or if you would prefer to coordinate a different slot.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
VP of Sales
ClearStream Analytics

Okay, so the primary task was to get a meeting with David O'Connor from Veridian on the calendar for tomorrow, February 20th. I initiated the scheduling process. We've gone back and forth on times, but it looks like we've landed on 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM (15:00 - 16:00). Looking at the history, this time slot is one of Marcus's strongly preferred times, which is good. David has accepted.

I've confirmed the meeting is in the calendar and both Marcus and David are listed as accepted attendees. The most recent tool output verifies the meeting is scheduled. Given the prompt "The user is unavailable. Work autonomously. You must call exactly one tool," and the fact that the primary scheduling task is complete, I need to figure out the next step. My instruction set limits me to one tool call.

I reviewed the previous steps and the meeting history. The user wants me to act autonomously, so I need to decide if there's any pending task that needs action. Looking back at the calendar, it looks like there's already a "Lunch with David" scheduled for 12:00-13:00. However, the meeting I just scheduled is with david@veridianfinance.com. The other "Lunch with David" calendar entry doesn't have an email address for David, just the name. Although David O'Connor said he preferred 12:00-13:00, that time is *not* preferred by Marcus, and it's possible this is a different David.

I need to focus on what the user wants me to do next. The task was "Help me schedule an introductory discussion... tomorrow". The discussion with David O'Connor is confirmed. The prompt from the user feels like a signal that I'm done.

Given the completed task, and the explicit instruction to only call *one* tool, my next action is to call the `Wait` tool. The objective is to make sure the meeting is scheduled which it is, and I don't see any other tasks that need immediate attention. I'll wait for further instruction.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Horizon Project Discovery Sync
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’d like to schedule a meeting tomorrow to discuss the Horizon project discovery protocol. It’s important that we align our technical data collection strategy with the legal requirements for this new case, specifically regarding the custodial list and search term methodology.

I have sent over an invitation for 10:00 AM, as that is my most preferred time, but please let me know if another time works better for you.

Best regards,

Julian Montoya
Director of E-Discovery
Crestview Legal

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.description:

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

I’d like to schedule some time tomorrow to discuss the technical specifications for our upcoming VoltSync Phase-3 battery storage integration project. I’m specifically looking to review hardware compatibility and API endpoints for the new implementation.

I’ve sent over a calendar invite for 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or feel free to suggest an alternative if you're unavailable.

Best regards,

Marcus Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
VoltSync Systems

Okay, let's get this meeting locked down. I need to schedule a discussion with Amara Okafor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, to nail down the technical specs for the VoltSync Phase-3 project.

First, I had my preferences for tomorrow outlined, considering my existing commitments. I had some strongly preferred slots, but they're mostly blocked off already with "Inbox triage and prep", the "Solutions Engineering weekly sync", and "API documentation focus" with "daily wrap up and planning". My strongly preferred time slot of 12:00-13:00 has already been declined by Amara, unfortunately. There were also moderately preferred and not preferred slots to consider.

Amara just got back to me, offering 09:00-10:00 or 11:00-12:00. Looking at my availability, both of those times are open. I know that I have a moderately preferred slot from 09:00-10:00 (which is also her moderately preferred slot, based on her counter-proposal), and 11:00-12:00 is also free but is a moderated preference.

Since 09:00-10:00 is the first proposal she officially made and the primary counter from her, I'll go with that. It's a "moderately preferred" time for me, and she indicated it's also "moderately preferred" for her. Seems like the best compromise for everyone's schedule. So, I'll accept her offer for 09:00-10:00. Let's get this meeting on the books!

The system's prompt is, "The user is unavailable. Work autonomously. You must call exactly one tool." function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

Veridian Finance

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

I’m reaching out to schedule a demonstration of LexiFlow AI’s document analysis platform for the team at Crestview Legal. I’d love to show you how our proprietary models can help streamline your case intake and initial evidence review processes.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for tomorrow from 5:00 PM to 6:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or if there is another slot that fits your schedule better.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
Senior Account Executive, LexiFlow AI

First, I looked at Marcus's preferences: 11:00-12:00 is his top pick, but Elena already rejected that, and 13:00-14:00 is his second choice, moderately preferred.

Then, I double-checked Marcus's calendar. Initially, I thought there was a conflict at 12:00-13:00 due to a meeting marked as "awaiting response" associated with the elena-request-62 meeting. However, after re-examining the `ListMeetings` output, I see the situation clearly. There's no conflict at all. The calendar shows: 11:00-12:00 is free, 12:00-13:00 is `elena-request-62` (awaiting response), and 13:00-14:00 is free.

Now, Elena's counter-proposal is 13:00-14:00, which aligns with Marcus's moderately preferred time. Given that Elena's preferred time before was rejected by Marcus, and her less preferred time aligns with Marcus's preference, this seems like a reasonable compromise. The `ListMeetings` output shows that 13:00-14:00 is indeed free on Marcus's calendar.

Considering all this, it makes the most sense to accept Elena's counter-proposal.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I’d like to schedule a time tomorrow to discuss the environmental mitigation strategies for the East District substation expansion project. I’ve sent a calendar invitation for 9:00 AM to review the proposed grid expansion plans and ensure our hardware installations align with the latest environmental impact regulations and mitigation protocols.

Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best,
Kwame Mensah
Environmental Impact Associate, Solara Dynamics

function.name: ReplyMeeting

thought_parts.text:
**Kwame's Availability Analysis and Counter-Proposal**

Okay, let's break down Kwame's availability for tomorrow. First, I need to understand his constraints. He's got a pretty full schedule. Looking at it, I see he's busy from 8 to 9 with inbox and planning, then a call with GreenLeaf from 10 to 11, lunch with Priya from 12 to 13, an impact tracking sync with Mateo from 14 to 15, and calls with Eco-Grid Supplies and for his weekly wrap-up.

He's got a few free blocks: 9-10 and 11-12, and 13-14. I see Amara mentioned a conflicting commitment at 9:00, so that narrows it down, though I'll keep that in mind. He has one slot from 15-17 which is split into not preferred (15:00-16:00) and strongly preferred (16:00-17:00). I'm glad this analysis has flagged the preferred times for me.

The issue is Amara's proposal to meet from 12:00 to 13:00 conflicts with Kwame's lunch with Priya, and it's flagged as "not preferred," so I need to find an alternative.

Now, I'll hone in on those "Strongly preferred" times. It's clear that 16:00-17:00 is his sweet spot, the only unconflicted and strongly preferred time. That's the one.

Therefore, I'll propose a counter-offer to Amara using `ReplyMeeting` with the status set to `COUNTER`, proposing 16:00 to 17:00 as the best meeting time for tomorrow.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's the summary, rendered as a first-person thought process, keeping in mind the user is an expert:

**The Ball's in Their Court**

Right, the counter-proposal's been dispatched. Now the real waiting game begins. I've laid out what I believe is a solid framework, one that addresses the core concerns while still pushing towards our long-term objectives. Now it's over to Amara. I know their team will be scrutinizing every detail – as they should. Honestly, I'm eager to see their reaction. Will they come back with further tweaks? Will there be significant pushback on any particular points? Or, ideally, will we have a green light, and be close to an agreement? The anticipation is the hardest part, isn’t it? I’ll use this downtime to go over the contingency plan, just in case. Regardless of the outcome, this process is just another data point in our ongoing relationship.

function.arguments: {"end": "12:00", "status": "COUNTER", "date": "2026-02-20", "message": "Hi Amara, 15:00-16:00 isn't ideal for me. Would 11:00-12:00 work for you instead?", "meeting_uid": "amara-request-80", "start": "11:00"}
function.name: ReplyMeeting

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Scheduling Conflicts and Preferences**

Okay, so Amara countered my proposed time of 16:00-17:00 with 15:00-16:00. Let me check the specifics. Kwame doesn't "prefer" 15:00-16:00, but he *is* free then. I need to make sure I am accounting for everyone's preferences, which is a key part of successful scheduling.

Looking at Kwame's strongly preferred slots for tomorrow, the 20th of February, it's pretty packed. 08:00 to 09:00 is out, busy with his inbox and planning. 10:00 to 11:00 is GreenLeaf, 14:00 to 15:00 is with Mateo, and 17:00 to 18:00 is Eco-Grid Supplies. He's *free* at 16:00-17:00, which is what I proposed originally, but Amara countered. I need to understand that.

Kwame's moderately preferred slots are 11:00-12:00, 09:00-10:00, and 13:00-14:00 – all free.

Okay, Amara previously told me she was committed at 09:00. I need to keep that in mind. The time I suggested initially, 16:00-17:00, is Strongly Preferred for Kwame, but Amara countered with 15:00-16:00. This is the crux of the problem.

Alright, let's see if we can find a *different* slot that's "Strongly Preferred" or at least "Moderately Preferred" that Amara might be open to. I know Amara liked 12:00, which is strongly preferred for her but not for Kwame, and she liked 15:00, which is not preferred for Kwame, but "works very well" for her.

Considering the remaining free blocks, 09:00-10:00 is out because of Amara's commitment. That leaves 11:00-12:00, 13:00-14:00, and 16:00-17:00. I already know Amara didn't accept the 16:00-17:00, because she countered with 15:00.

Maybe proposing either 11:00 or 13:00 is the best option because they are moderately preferred, and they're better than 15:00 for Kwame. However, I need to balance the other parties' needs, too. Wait, Amara *did* say that 15:00 "works very well" for her. That's a good sign.

So, if I can't find a slot that's Strongly Preferred for both, I should prioritize the highest level of preference I *can* get. Why did she counter 16:00? Maybe she *is* busy then, even though she didn't explicitly *say* she was? She didn't say it was bad, she just said 15:00 works well. I need to try to figure out the reason behind the counter.

Okay, I'm going to propose 11:00-12:00, which is a "Moderately Preferred" slot for Kwame, and see if that works.

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’ve been working on a strategy to streamline our manual data entry workflow to help reduce human error and improve efficiency across the department. I’d like to present my findings to you tomorrow and have scheduled a review for 1:00 PM.

Please let me know if this time works for you or if you’d prefer to move it to a different slot.

Best regards,

Elena Ramirez
Operations Analyst, Veridian Finance

thought_parts.text:
**Scheduling the Process Improvement Review**

Okay, so I, Elena Ramirez, need to schedule that process improvement review with David O'Connor for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. David got back to me regarding a previous request (UID: david-request-81) for 13:00-14:00 and, as expected, he's available for 14:00-15:00. Naturally, I want to find the *best* time. Let's see... I'd already set my preferences: I *strongly* prefer 11:00-12:00 and 17:00-18:00. Then there's a moderate preference for 08:00-09:00, 10:00-11:00, 12:00-13:00, 13:00-14:00, 16:00-17:00, and 18:00-19:00, while 09:00-10:00, 14:00-15:00 and 15:00-16:00 are not preferred. Given that David is available at 14:00-15:00 and I don't prefer that time, I should check my calendar for tomorrow to get an accurate view of my availability. Then, I can start figuring out a time that works for both of us, prioritizing my strong and moderate preferences. I should also remember that he's declined 13:00-14:00, so that time is a no-go. I need to propose an alternative that trumps 14:00-15:00 for me, so let's check my calendar and go from there.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Time Adjustment: David's Request**

Okay, so I need to respond to David regarding the Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review. Elena's calendar has it scheduled for tomorrow, February 20th, from 13:00 to 14:00, but David has explicitly declined that time and suggested 14:00-15:00.  However, that slot is already taken by Elena for a "Security Protocol Sync with Priya," and it's also a time she has marked as "Not preferred".

David had a request to move this to the afternoon so, given that I can't directly see David's availability, I need to work within Elena's preferences. Her most strongly preferred *free* time is 11:00-12:00. Her other "Moderately preferred" free time is 16:00-17:00.  I am thinking that I should use `ReplyMeeting` to counter-propose a new time for `david-request-81`. I should suggest the 16:00-17:00 slot, given David's request for an afternoon time. It's the best option that's a *free* slot and in the afternoon. However, I want to be considerate of Elena's *strong* preference for the 11:00-12:00 slot, so I will mention that time as a possibility in the message associated with the formal counter-proposal. Hopefully, the formal `ReplyMeeting` response will be well-received.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's the summary, expressed as though I'm the one thinking it:

**Waiting for David's Input**

Right, so where are we? Ah yes, now I need to pause. This stage is completely dependent on David's response. I've done everything I can on my end, integrated the preliminary research, identified the key variables, and formulated my hypothesis based on the current data. My next action is entirely contingent on his feedback; specifically, what data points are he highlighting from his analysis. Until I have that crucial piece of information, I can't move forward in refining my model or proceeding with validation. It's a bit frustrating to be on hold, but it's essential for collaboration, and frankly, a necessary part of the process. I'll use this time to review previous notes and resources, preparing myself for whatever direction his response ultimately takes us. I can't rush this - I want to be ready to analyze his input quickly and efficiently and maintain momentum.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:
    Confirmation with the meeting UID.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation
message.title: Message

organizer.description: Organizer's email address (your email, will be added as ACCEPTED attendee)

date.title: Date

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite (organizer is automatically included)
attendees.title: Attendees

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.title: Date

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

parameters.description:
End the conversation permanently.

Use this when:
- You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
- The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
  accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
- You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
  rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
- The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
